sourceRef: tip:Three questions to ask before certifying a non-standard term

slides.headline: Three Questions Before Certifying Non-Standard Terms
slides.body: Master summer session certifications with precision.
bullets: Ensure all terms align with Chapter 33

slides.headline: Verify Eligibility
slides.body: Check if the student qualifies for Chapter 33.
bullets: Confirm the student’s eligibility based on the form

slides.headline: Check Summer Sessions
slides.body: Ensure the term matches the summer session.
bullets: Verify the term is a summer session
Refer to your state’s summer session guidelines

slides.headline: Review COE
slides.body: Examine the student’s Certificate of Eligibility.
bullets: Check if the term aligns with the student’s COE
Refer to the student’s 85/15 rule eligibility

slides.headline: Cross-Reference
slides.body: Double-check with your State Approving Agency (SAA) letter.
bullets: Consult your SAA letter for guidance
Confirm any special rules or exceptions

slides.headline: Stay Updated
slides.body: Stay informed about the latest Chapter 33 updates.
bullets: Refer to the relevant VA guidance

slides.body: Explore more tips for VASCOs. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Master the art of certifying non-standard terms with these three crucial questions. Don’t miss out on subtle mistakes in summer sessions. Explore more tips for VASCOs. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is the last one, which lacks a clear takeaway and feels like an afterthought.
